action.reasoning:
Observation: All variants of u/(d+c) score 0.57-0.59, with utility/(d+1) being best at 0.5918. Variance ~±0.015. 14 iters left, 2.6h, eval ~12min → ~10 evals max. Room success ~76% is the bottleneck. Need a more impactful change.

Analysis: Small tweaks to scoring function aren't moving the needle. The real issue is that the planner sometimes gets stuck/oscillates in rooms. A more impactful change: when no useful target is found locally, the fallback (nearest unvisited) may cause backtracking. Better fallback: go to NEAREST FRONTIER directly (not nearest unvisited node). Also, I should commit to a target with persistence to avoid oscillation.

Let me try a substantive improvement: 'sweep' mode — when explored_rate is high (>0.85), go to NEAREST utility>0 node (greedy completion); otherwise use u/(d+1) for efficient global exploration. This addresses the late-stage cleanup which is when timeouts happen.

Decision: Implement two-phase strategy: early use u/(d+1), late (explored>0.85) use 1/(d+1) — i.e., go to nearest frontier. Also add target persistence to reduce zigzag. Submit eval.
